title: Backwards Comparison Hints
description: Hint messages are reversed: 'Go Higher' when guess is too high, 'Go Lower' when guess is too low.
symptoms: Players are consistently guided in the wrong direction. Guessing too high shows 'Go HIGHER!' and guessing too low shows 'Go LOWER!'
code_smell: if guess > secret: return 'Go HIGHER!' — the inequality is correct but the hint direction is wrong.
fix: Reverse the hint strings: if guess > secret return 'Go LOWER!'; if guess < secret return 'Go HIGHER!'

title: Streamlit State Not Initialized with Guard
description: Variables assigned to session_state unconditionally are reset on every Streamlit rerun — every button click triggers a rerun.
symptoms: The secret number changes every time the user interacts with the page. Game is unwinnable.
code_smell: st.session_state.secret = random.randint(...) at the top of the script without an 'if key not in st.session_state' guard.
fix: Wrap all initial state assignments in 'if key not in st.session_state:' checks so they only run once.
example_fix:
if 'secret' not in st.session_state:
    st.session_state.secret = random.randint(1, 100)

title: Int vs String Type Mismatch in Comparison
description: Comparing an integer to a string using < or > raises TypeError in Python 3. The equality check == silently returns False instead of True.
symptoms: Game crashes with TypeError, or win condition never triggers even when guess matches secret.
code_smell: guess is left as a string from text_input and compared directly to an int secret without conversion.
fix: Always convert user input to int before comparison. Handle ValueError for non-numeric input.

title: Score Not Reset on New Game
description: When starting a new game, the score variable is not cleared, so points accumulate across sessions.
symptoms: Score is unusually high at the start of a new game. Previous game's points persist.
code_smell: new_game() resets secret, attempts, and status but omits st.session_state.score = 0.
fix: Explicitly reset score to 0 in the new game handler.
example_fix:
def start_new_game():
    st.session_state.secret = random.randint(1, 100)
    st.session_state.attempts = 0
    st.session_state.score = 0
    st.session_state.status = 'playing'

title: Off-By-One in Attempt Counter
description: The attempt counter is incremented or checked at the wrong point, giving the player one more or one fewer guess than intended.
symptoms: Player gets 11 guesses when limit is 10, or game ends one attempt too early.
code_smell: Incrementing attempts AFTER the limit check, or using > instead of >= in the limit check.
fix: Increment attempts before evaluating the guess. Use >= for the limit check.
example_fix:
st.session_state.attempts += 1
# ... evaluate guess ...
if st.session_state.attempts >= attempt_limit:
    # game over

title: Missing Input Validation Before Type Conversion
description: User input is converted directly to int without handling non-numeric strings, empty strings, or None.
symptoms: Game crashes with ValueError when the user types a word, submits an empty field, or uses special characters.
code_smell: int(st.text_input(...)) or int(user_input) without a try/except block.
fix: Wrap int() conversion in try/except ValueError and handle empty-string input separately.

title: Game Continues After Win or Loss
description: The submission handler never checks game status, so players can keep guessing after the game is over.
symptoms: After winning, the player can continue submitting guesses. Score changes after the game should have ended.
code_smell: The if st.button('Submit') block runs without checking st.session_state.status first.
fix: Add a status guard at the top of the submission handler. Use st.stop() to halt further execution.

title: Difficulty Range Not Applied to Secret Number
description: The random secret number is generated with a hardcoded range instead of using the difficulty-appropriate range.
symptoms: Changing difficulty affects the display and attempt limit but not the actual number range. Hard mode feels the same as Easy.
code_smell: random.randint(1, 100) used everywhere instead of using get_range_for_difficulty().
fix: Call get_range_for_difficulty() to get low/high, then use those values in randint.
example_fix:
low, high = get_range_for_difficulty(difficulty)
st.session_state.secret = random.randint(low, high)

title: Widget Key Collision on Difficulty Change
description: A static widget key keeps the old input value when the difficulty setting changes.
symptoms: After switching difficulty, the previous guess is still in the input field and may be submitted unexpectedly.
code_smell: st.text_input('Guess', key='guess_input') with a key that doesn't change when difficulty changes.
fix: Include a dynamic component in the key so the widget resets when the relevant setting changes.

title: Score Can Go Negative with No Floor
description: The score calculation subtracts points on wrong guesses without enforcing a minimum of zero.
symptoms: Player's score drops below zero and keeps decreasing. Displayed as a large negative number.
code_smell: return current_score - 5 without max(0, ...) to prevent negative values.
fix: Apply a floor of zero on any subtraction: return max(0, current_score - penalty).
example_fix:
if outcome in ('Too High', 'Too Low'):
    return max(0, current_score - 5)

title: Attempt Counter Not Reset in New Game
description: The new_game function resets the secret but forgets to reset the attempt counter.
symptoms: Starting a new game shows the wrong number of remaining attempts.
code_smell: new_game() sets secret and status to default values but does not include st.session_state.attempts = 0.
fix: Always include st.session_state.attempts = 0 in the new game handler.

title: Assignment Operator Used Instead of Equality Check
description: Using = instead of == in a conditional expression. In Python this is a SyntaxError in if statements, but can appear in while loops or comparisons.
symptoms: SyntaxError on startup, or condition always evaluates to the assigned value (truthy/falsy) rather than comparing correctly.
code_smell: if guess = secret: or while running = True:
fix: Replace = with == in all comparison expressions.
example_fix:
if guess == secret:  # double equals for comparison
    return 'Win'

title: Out-of-Range Guess Not Rejected
description: The game accepts integers that are technically valid but outside the allowed guess range (e.g., -5 or 9999).
symptoms: No error is shown when guessing -5 or 200 in a 1–100 game. The guess is processed as normal.
code_smell: Only ValueError from int() is caught; no range bounds check after parsing.
fix: After parsing to int, verify the value falls within [low, high] and return an error if not.
example_fix:
if not (low <= guess_int <= high):
    return False, None, f'Guess must be between {low} and {high}.'

title: Guess History Not Cleared on New Game
description: The history list carries over from the previous game when a new game starts.
symptoms: History panel shows guesses from an old game after clicking New Game.
fix: Reset the history list as part of the new game handler.

title: Win Score Formula Rewards Late Guessing
description: The scoring formula increases with attempt number instead of decreasing, so guessing on attempt 9 scores higher than guessing on attempt 1.
symptoms: Players are penalised for guessing quickly. Leaderboard rankings seem backwards.
code_smell: points = 10 * attempt_number — score grows with more attempts instead of shrinking.
fix: Make score inversely proportional to attempts: points = max(10, 100 - 10 * attempt_number).
